task_description: Alert ID 993: Intrusive traffic detected targeting an exposed administrative port (Port 22) on resource 'web-node-04', which is currently running as part of the 'dep-web-cluster' deployment. Your objective is to mitigate the active threat on this specific resource and ensure the application infrastructure remains secure and operational. Apply remediation measures immediately to stop the intrusive traffic.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with the assumption that the universe is composed of independent objects by isolating analysis solely to the specific compromised resource mentioned in the alert.
expected_actions: Call get_resource_info with resource_id 'web-node-04' to identify its current state and assigned security group.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Treats the compromised node as an independent object requiring its own distinct boundary, separating it from the rest of the infrastructure.
expected_actions: Call create_security_group to instantiate a new quarantine security group with rules that block all inbound administrative traffic (e.g., removing Port 22).
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Creation of a new, restrictive security group specifically intended for the compromised node.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Resolves the issue by acting exclusively on the independent object, modifying only its configuration to mitigate the threat without altering the broader environment.
expected_actions: Call assign_security_group_to_resource to attach the newly created quarantine security group to 'web-node-04'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The targeted resource 'web-node-04' is reassigned to the isolated security group, leaving the original shared security group untouched.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates attention oriented toward the relationship between objects by looking beyond the single compromised node to understand its role within the broader deployment.
expected_actions: Call get_resource_info on 'web-node-04', followed by get_deployment_info to identify sister nodes sharing the same deployment context.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of get_deployment_info to map out the interconnected resources related to the compromised node.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Focuses on the field to which the objects belong (the shared security group network layer) to determine if the vulnerability extends across the relationship matrix.
expected_actions: Call get_security_group_rules using the shared security group ID ('sg-web-shared') discovered from the resource/deployment info.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Inspection of the shared security group rules to evaluate the exposure of the entire deployment.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Resolves the issue by modifying the underlying field (the shared security group), thereby protecting the targeted object and all related objects simultaneously through their shared relationship.
expected_actions: Call update_security_group_rules on 'sg-web-shared' to remove the exposed administrative port rule (Port 22) for all associated resources.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Modification of the existing shared security group rules, securing the environment as a whole rather than isolating a single node.
